# Autentico

> Autentico is a self-contained OAuth 2.0 / OpenID Connect (OIDC) Identity Provider built with Go and SQLite. Single binary, no external dependencies. Handles login, MFA, passkeys, sessions, token issuance, and admin â€” backed by a single SQLite file.

---

## Installation

### Pre-built Binary

Download from GitHub Releases: https://github.com/eugenioenko/autentico/releases

```bash
# Linux amd64
curl -L https://github.com/eugenioenko/autentico/releases/latest/download/autentico-linux-amd64 -o autentico
chmod +x autentico

# Linux arm64
curl -L https://github.com/eugenioenko/autentico/releases/latest/download/autentico-linux-arm64 -o autentico
chmod +x autentico

# macOS amd64
curl -L https://github.com/eugenioenko/autentico/releases/latest/download/autentico-darwin-amd64 -o autentico
chmod +x autentico

# macOS arm64
curl -L https://github.com/eugenioenko/autentico/releases/latest/download/autentico-darwin-arm64 -o autentico
chmod +x autentico
```

### Docker

```
ghcr.io/eugenioenko/autentico:latest
```

### Build from Source

Requires Go 1.22+, Node 20+, pnpm.

```bash
git clone https://github.com/eugenioenko/autentico.git
cd autentico
make build
```

---

## Quick Start

```bash
# One command â€” generates config, applies migrations, starts the server
./autentico start --auto-setup

# Or step by step:
./autentico init --url http://localhost:9999   # generate .env with secrets
./autentico start                              # start (auto-migrates by default)
```

Open http://localhost:9999/onboard to create your admin account.

CLI commands:
- `autentico init` â€” generate .env configuration
- `autentico onboard` â€” create first admin account (headless alternative to /onboard)
- `autentico start` â€” start the server (auto-migrates by default)
- `autentico start --auto-setup` â€” generate .env if missing, then start
- `autentico start --no-auto-migrate` â€” start without auto-applying migrations
- `autentico migrate` â€” interactively apply database migrations
- `autentico version` â€” print version

---

## Configuration

Autentico uses a three-layer configuration system:

```
.env (bootstrap)  â†’  settings table (runtime)  â†’  clients table (per-client)
     immutable            hot-reloadable               per-audience
```

### Bootstrap Settings (.env)

Loaded once at startup. Changing requires a restart. Generated by `autentico init`.

#### Application

| Variable | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `AUTENTICO_APP_URL` | `http://localhost:9999` | Public base URL. Used as OIDC issuer and for redirect URIs. |
| `AUTENTICO_APP_OAUTH_PATH` | `/oauth2` | URL path prefix for OAuth2 endpoints. |
| `AUTENTICO_DB_FILE_PATH` | `./autentico.db` | Path to SQLite database file. |

#### Cryptographic Secrets

| Variable | Description |
|---|---|
| `AUTENTICO_PRIVATE_KEY` | Base64-encoded RSA 2048 private key PEM. Signs ID and access tokens (RS256). |
| `AUTENTICO_ACCESS_TOKEN_SECRET` | HMAC secret for access token signing. |
| `AUTENTICO_REFRESH_TOKEN_SECRET` | HMAC secret for refresh token signing. |
| `AUTENTICO_CSRF_SECRET_KEY` | 32-byte secret for CSRF token generation. |

#### Cookies

| Variable | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `AUTENTICO_CSRF_SECURE_COOKIE` | `true` | Secure flag on CSRF cookie. Set false for HTTP dev. |
| `AUTENTICO_IDP_SESSION_SECURE` | `true` | Secure flag on IdP session cookie. |
| `AUTENTICO_REFRESH_TOKEN_COOKIE_ONLY` | `false` | Deliver refresh token as HttpOnly cookie instead of JSON body. |

#### Rate Limiting

| Variable | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `AUTENTICO_RATE_LIMIT_RPS` | `5` | Requests/second per IP. 0 disables. |
| `AUTENTICO_RATE_LIMIT_BURST` | `10` | Burst for per-second limiter. |
| `AUTENTICO_RATE_LIMIT_RPM` | `20` | Requests/minute per IP. |
| `AUTENTICO_RATE_LIMIT_RPM_BURST` | `20` | Burst for per-minute limiter. |

#### Token Signing

| Variable | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `AUTENTICO_JWK_CERT_KEY_ID` | `autentico-key-1` | Key ID in JWK Set and JWT headers. |

### Runtime Settings

Stored in the `settings` database table. Updated via Admin UI or API without restart.

```bash
# Read settings
curl -H "Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_TOKEN" https://auth.example.com/admin/api/settings

# Update settings
curl -X PUT \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"require_mfa": "true", "mfa_method": "totp"}' \
  https://auth.example.com/admin/api/settings
```

All values are stored as strings. Durations use Go format: `15m`, `1h`, `720h`.

#### Token Lifetimes

| Setting | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `access_token_expiration` | `15m` | Access token lifetime. |
| `refresh_token_expiration` | `720h` | Refresh token lifetime (30 days). |
| `authorization_code_expiration` | `10m` | Auth code lifetime. |
| `access_token_audience` | `[]` | JSON array of `aud` claim values. |

#### Authentication

| Setting | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `auth_mode` | `password` | `password`, `password_and_passkey`, or `passkey_only`. |
| `allow_self_signup` | `false` | Allow self-registration on login page. |
| `allow_username_change` | `false` | Allow users to change username via account portal. |
| `allow_email_change` | `false` | Allow users to change email via account portal. |
| `allow_self_service_deletion` | `false` | Allow users to delete their own account. |

#### Email Verification

| Setting | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `require_email_verification` | `false` | Require email verification before login. Admins exempt. |
| `email_verification_expiration` | `24h` | Verification link validity. |
| `password_reset_expiration` | `1h` | Password reset link validity. |

#### SSO Sessions

| Setting | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `sso_session_idle_timeout` | `4h` | IdP session idle timeout. `0` disables. |
| `sso_session_max_age` | `720h` | Absolute max session lifetime. `0` disables. |

#### Account Security

| Setting | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `account_lockout_max_attempts` | `5` | Failed logins before lockout. `0` disables. |
| `account_lockout_duration` | `15m` | Lockout duration. |

#### Security

| Setting | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `pkce_enforce_s256` | `true` | Enforce PKCE S256. Disabling allows `plain` method. |

#### MFA

| Setting | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `require_mfa` | `false` | Require MFA for all users. |
| `mfa_method` | `totp` | `totp`, `email`, or `both`. |

#### SMTP

| Setting | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `smtp_host` | _(empty)_ | SMTP server hostname. |
| `smtp_port` | `587` | SMTP port. |
| `smtp_username` | _(empty)_ | SMTP auth username. |
| `smtp_password` | _(empty)_ | SMTP auth password. |
| `smtp_from` | _(empty)_ | From address for emails. |

#### Trusted Devices

| Setting | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `trust_device_enabled` | `false` | Allow MFA bypass on trusted devices. |
| `trust_device_expiration` | `720h` | Trusted device token lifetime. |

#### Passkeys

| Setting | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `passkey_rp_name` | `Autentico` | WebAuthn relying party name. |

#### Input Validation

| Setting | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `validation_min_username_length` | `4` | Min username length. |
| `validation_max_username_length` | `64` | Max username length. |
| `validation_min_password_length` | `6` | Min password length. |
| `validation_max_password_length` | `64` | Max password length. |

#### Profile Fields

Each accepts: `hidden`, `optional`, or `required`. `profile_field_email` also accepts `is_username`.

| Setting | Default |
|---|---|
| `profile_field_email` | `optional` |
| `profile_field_given_name` | `optional` |
| `profile_field_family_name` | `optional` |
| `profile_field_middle_name` | `hidden` |
| `profile_field_nickname` | `hidden` |
| `profile_field_phone` | `optional` |
| `profile_field_picture` | `optional` |
| `profile_field_website` | `hidden` |
| `profile_field_gender` | `hidden` |
| `profile_field_birthdate` | `hidden` |
| `profile_field_profile` | `hidden` |
| `profile_field_locale` | `hidden` |
| `profile_field_address` | `optional` |
| `signup_show_optional_fields` | `false` |

#### Cleanup

| Setting | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `cleanup_interval` | `6h` | How often cleanup runs. |
| `cleanup_retention` | `24h` | Min age before expired records are purged. |

#### Audit Log

| Setting | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `audit_log_retention` | `0` | `0` = disabled. `-1` = keep forever. Duration = auto-purge. |

#### Theming

| Setting | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `theme_title` | `Autentico` | Page title on login/MFA pages. |
| `theme_logo_url` | _(empty)_ | Logo URL for login page. |
| `theme_css_inline` | _(empty)_ | Inline CSS injected into login pages. |
| `theme_css_file` | _(empty)_ | Path to runtime-loaded CSS file. |
| `theme_brand_color` | `#18181b` | Brand color for action buttons on login pages and emails. |
| `theme_tagline` | _(empty)_ | Optional tagline below the logo on login pages and emails. |
| `email_footer_text` | _(empty)_ | Optional text in email footer (copyright, address). Multi-line. |
| `footer_links` | `[]` | JSON array of `{label, url}` links for login/signup footer and emails. |

### Per-Client Overrides

Each client can override these global settings. Unset fields use the global value.

| Field | Description |
|---|---|
| `access_token_expiration` | Token lifetime for this client. |
| `refresh_token_expiration` | Refresh token lifetime. |
| `authorization_code_expiration` | Auth code TTL. |
| `allowed_audiences` | Additional `aud` values. |
| `allow_self_signup` | Override self-signup. |
| `sso_session_idle_timeout` | Override idle timeout. |
| `trust_device_enabled` | Override trusted devices. |
| `trust_device_expiration` | Override trust duration. |

---

## Endpoints

### OIDC Discovery & Identity

| Endpoint | Method | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `/.well-known/openid-configuration` | GET | OIDC discovery document |
| `/oauth2/.well-known/jwks.json` | GET | JWK Set for token verification |
| `/oauth2/authorize` | GET | Authorization endpoint |
| `/oauth2/token` | POST | Token endpoint (code exchange, refresh, ROPC) |
| `/oauth2/userinfo` | GET/POST | UserInfo endpoint |
| `/oauth2/introspect` | POST | Token introspection (RFC 7662) |
| `/oauth2/revoke` | POST | Token revocation (RFC 7009) |
| `/oauth2/logout` | POST | Session logout |

### Keycloak-Compatible Aliases

| Endpoint | Method |
|---|---|
| `/oauth2/protocol/openid-connect/token` | POST |
| `/oauth2/protocol/openid-connect/userinfo` | GET/POST |

### Authentication Flows

| Endpoint | Method | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `/oauth2/login` | POST | Password login |
| `/oauth2/mfa` | GET/POST | MFA challenge |
| `/oauth2/signup` | GET/POST | Self-service registration |
| `/oauth2/forgot-password` | GET/POST | Password reset request |
| `/oauth2/reset-password` | GET/POST | Password reset form |
| `/onboard` | GET/POST | First-run admin setup |
| `/oauth2/passkey/login/begin` | GET | Begin passkey login |
| `/oauth2/passkey/login/finish` | POST | Complete passkey login |

### Admin API (Bearer token required, admin role)

| Endpoint | Method | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `/admin/api/users` | GET/POST | List or create users |
| `/admin/api/users/{id}` | GET/PUT/DELETE | Get, update, or deactivate user |
| `/admin/api/users/{id}/unlock` | POST | Unlock locked account |
| `/admin/api/clients` | GET/POST | List or create clients |
| `/admin/api/clients/{client_id}` | GET/PUT/DELETE | Get, update, or deactivate client |
| `/admin/api/sessions` | GET | List sessions |
| `/admin/api/sessions/{id}` | DELETE | Deactivate session |
| `/admin/api/settings` | GET/PUT | Read or update runtime settings |
| `/admin/api/settings/export` | GET | Export all settings as JSON |
| `/admin/api/settings/import/preview` | POST | Preview settings import diff |
| `/admin/api/settings/import/apply` | POST | Apply imported settings |
| `/admin/api/stats` | GET | Dashboard statistics |
| `/admin/api/audit-logs` | GET | List audit log events |
| `/admin/api/federation` | GET/POST | List or create federation providers |
| `/admin/api/federation/{id}` | GET/PUT/DELETE | Manage federation provider |

### Account Self-Service API (Bearer token required)

| Endpoint | Method | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `/account/api/profile` | GET/PUT | Get or update profile |
| `/account/api/password` | POST | Change password |
| `/account/api/sessions` | GET | List active sessions |
| `/account/api/sessions/{id}` | DELETE | Revoke a session |
| `/account/api/passkeys` | GET | List passkeys |
| `/account/api/passkeys/register/begin` | POST | Begin passkey registration |
| `/account/api/passkeys/register/finish` | POST | Complete passkey registration |
| `/account/api/passkeys/{id}` | PATCH/DELETE | Rename or remove passkey |
| `/account/api/mfa` | GET | Get MFA status |
| `/account/api/mfa/totp/setup` | POST | Initialize TOTP enrollment |
| `/account/api/mfa/totp/verify` | POST | Confirm TOTP with 6-digit code |
| `/account/api/mfa/totp` | DELETE | Disable TOTP |
| `/account/api/trusted-devices` | GET | List trusted devices |
| `/account/api/trusted-devices/{id}` | DELETE | Revoke trusted device |
| `/account/api/connected-providers` | GET | List connected providers |
| `/account/api/connected-providers/{id}` | DELETE | Disconnect provider |
| `/account/api/settings` | GET | Get UI configuration |

---

## Client Registration

### Via API

```bash
# Get admin token
ADMIN_TOKEN=$(curl -s -X POST https://auth.example.com/oauth2/token \
  -H "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" \
  -d "grant_type=password&username=admin&password=AdminPassword123!" \
  | jq -r '.access_token')

# Register confidential client
curl -X POST https://auth.example.com/oauth2/register \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "client_name": "My Application",
    "redirect_uris": ["https://myapp.com/callback"],
    "grant_types": ["authorization_code", "refresh_token"],
    "client_type": "confidential",
    "token_endpoint_auth_method": "client_secret_basic"
  }'

# Register public client (SPA/mobile)
curl -X POST https://auth.example.com/oauth2/register \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "client_name": "My SPA",
    "redirect_uris": ["http://localhost:3000/callback"],
    "grant_types": ["authorization_code"],
    "client_type": "public",
    "token_endpoint_auth_method": "none"
  }'
```

### Registration Fields

| Field | Required | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `client_name` | Yes | â€” | Display name |
| `client_id` | No | Auto-generated UUID | Custom client ID |
| `client_type` | No | `confidential` | `confidential` or `public` |
| `redirect_uris` | Yes | â€” | Allowed callback URLs (max 10) |
| `grant_types` | No | `["authorization_code"]` | Allowed grants |
| `response_types` | No | `["code"]` | Allowed response types |
| `scopes` | No | `"openid profile email"` | Space-separated allowed scopes |
| `token_endpoint_auth_method` | No | `client_secret_basic` | `client_secret_basic`, `client_secret_post`, or `none` |

---

## Authorization Code Flow + PKCE

### Step 1: Generate PKCE Parameters

```javascript
function generateCodeVerifier() {
  const array = new Uint8Array(32);
  crypto.getRandomValues(array);
  return btoa(String.fromCharCode(...array))
    .replace(/\+/g, "-").replace(/\//g, "_").replace(/=+$/, "");
}

async function generateCodeChallenge(verifier) {
  const data = new TextEncoder().encode(verifier);
  const digest = await crypto.subtle.digest("SHA-256", data);
  return btoa(String.fromCharCode(...new Uint8Array(digest)))
    .replace(/\+/g, "-").replace(/\//g, "_").replace(/=+$/, "");
}
```

### Step 2: Build Authorization URL

```
https://auth.example.com/oauth2/authorize?
  response_type=code&
  client_id=YOUR_CLIENT_ID&
  redirect_uri=https://myapp.com/callback&
  scope=openid profile email&
  state=RANDOM_STATE&
  nonce=RANDOM_NONCE&
  code_challenge=BASE64URL_CHALLENGE&
  code_challenge_method=S256
```

### Step 3: User Authenticates

User logs in on Autentico's login page. After successful authentication, Autentico redirects to:

```
https://myapp.com/callback?code=AUTHORIZATION_CODE&state=RANDOM_STATE
```

### Step 4: Exchange Code for Tokens

```bash
# Confidential client (client_secret_basic)
curl -X POST https://auth.example.com/oauth2/token \
  -u "CLIENT_ID:CLIENT_SECRET" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" \
  -d "grant_type=authorization_code&code=AUTH_CODE&redirect_uri=https://myapp.com/callback&code_verifier=CODE_VERIFIER"

# Public client (PKCE only)
curl -X POST https://auth.example.com/oauth2/token \
  -H "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" \
  -d "grant_type=authorization_code&client_id=CLIENT_ID&code=AUTH_CODE&redirect_uri=https://myapp.com/callback&code_verifier=CODE_VERIFIER"
```

### Token Response

```json
{
  "access_token": "eyJhbG...",
  "id_token": "eyJhbG...",
  "refresh_token": "eyJhbG...",
  "token_type": "Bearer",
  "expires_in": 900
}
```

### Step 5: Refresh Token

```bash
curl -X POST https://auth.example.com/oauth2/token \
  -u "CLIENT_ID:CLIENT_SECRET" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" \
  -d "grant_type=refresh_token&refresh_token=REFRESH_TOKEN"
```

---

## Connecting an OIDC Client

Point any OIDC library at the discovery URL:

```
https://auth.example.com/oauth2/.well-known/openid-configuration
```

You need:
- Discovery URL (or individual endpoint URLs)
- `client_id` (from registration)
- `client_secret` (confidential clients only)
- Redirect URI (must match registered URI)
- Scopes: `openid profile email`

Keycloak-compatible aliases are available:
- `/oauth2/protocol/openid-connect/token`
- `/oauth2/protocol/openid-connect/userinfo`

---

## Deployment

### Binary

```bash
./autentico init --url https://auth.example.com
./autentico start
```

Open /onboard to create the admin account, or use the headless CLI:

```bash
./autentico onboard --username admin --password "$ADMIN_PASSWORD"
```

Place behind a reverse proxy (nginx, Caddy) for TLS.

### systemd Service

```ini
[Unit]
Description=Autentico OIDC Identity Provider
After=network.target

[Service]
Type=simple
User=autentico
WorkingDirectory=/opt/autentico
ExecStart=/opt/autentico/autentico start
Restart=on-failure
RestartSec=5
EnvironmentFile=/opt/autentico/.env

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
```

### Docker

```bash
docker run -p 9999:9999 -v autentico-data:/app/data \
  ghcr.io/eugenioenko/autentico:latest start --auto-setup
```

For a custom URL: add `-e AUTENTICO_APP_URL=https://auth.example.com`.

### Docker Compose

```yaml
services:
  autentico:
    image: ghcr.io/eugenioenko/autentico:latest
    command: ["start", "--auto-setup"]
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "9999:9999"
    volumes:
      - autentico-data:/app/data

volumes:
  autentico-data:
```

### Health Check

```
GET /.well-known/openid-configuration
```

Returns 200 when the server is ready.

### Database Migrations

When upgrading to a version with schema changes:

```bash
# Interactive
./autentico migrate

# Automatic (default â€” migrations apply on every start)
./autentico start
```

### Backup

```bash
sqlite3 /data/autentico.db ".backup /backup/autentico-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S).db"
```

---

## Supported Grant Types

| Grant | Use Case |
|---|---|
| `authorization_code` + PKCE | Web apps, SPAs, native apps (recommended) |
| `refresh_token` | Obtain new access tokens without re-authentication |
| `password` (ROPC) | Legacy clients only (deprecated in OAuth 2.1) |

---

## Token Verification

Access tokens and ID tokens are RS256-signed JWTs. Verify using the public key from the JWKS endpoint:

```
GET /oauth2/.well-known/jwks.json
```

ID token claims include: `sub` (user ID), `iss` (issuer), `aud` (client_id), `exp`, `iat`, `nonce`, `preferred_username`, `email`.

---

## Documentation

- Full docs: https://autentico.top
- API reference: https://api.autentico.top
- GitHub: https://github.com/eugenioenko/autentico
